name: Triple-Rich Chocolate Squares
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Redbook January 1995
Heat oven to 350 degrees F; line a 9-inch square baking pan with foil, leaving 2 inches overhangs on all sides.
In a food processor or blender, process the Oreos until they are fine crumbs. You should have about 1-1/2 cups of crumbs. Transfer the crumbs to the prepared baking pan and press firmly over the bottom. Set aside.
In a large bowl with an electric mixer set to high speed, beat together the cream cheese, sugar, chocolate drink powder, flour, eggs, and vanilla until just blended and smooth.
Pour the mixture evenly over the crust in the pan.
Bake for 25 minutes or until set but still soft in the center. Let cool completely on a wire rack.
Refrigerate, covered, at least 3 hours or overnight.
At serving time, melt chocolate chips and shortening in a small microwave-safe bowl on HIGH for 1 to 2 minutes.
Dip the tip of a spoon into the melted chocolate and drizzle over the chilled square in a random pattern.
Using the foil overhangs as handles, lift the square from the pan. Peel away the foil from the sides of the square and cut into 2-inch squares.
